_metadata.description: Seed data for the continuity_families reference table

data.name: Generation 1
data.notes: Original 1984 continuity family. Encompasses NA cartoon (Seasons 1-4), the 1986 movie, Japanese series (Headmasters, Masterforce, Victory, Zone), G2, all comic publishers (Marvel US/UK, Dreamwave, IDW Phase 1 & 2, Skybound/Energon Universe), Binaltech, and Classics. Toy-only G1 characters also belong here.

data.name: Beast Era
data.notes: Beast Wars (1996-1999) and Beast Machines (1999-2000). Separate family because character names are reused for entirely different characters (Beast Wars Megatron is not G1 Megatron).

data.name: Robots in Disguise (2001)
data.notes: Car Robots (2000) / Robots in Disguise (2001). Japanese series adapted for Western audiences. Standalone continuity.

data.name: Unicron Trilogy
data.notes: Armada (2002-2003), Energon (2004-2005), Cybertron (2005-2006). Shared continuity across three series with distinct character designs.

data.name: Live-Action Movies
data.notes: Live-action film series. Bay films (2007-2017), Bumblebee (2018), Rise of the Beasts (2023). Distinct character designs per film.

data.name: Transformers Animated
data.notes: Transformers Animated (2007-2009). Stylized standalone continuity set in futuristic Detroit.

data.name: Aligned / Prime
data.notes: Aligned continuity family. War for Cybertron / Fall of Cybertron games, Transformers Prime (2010-2013), Rescue Bots (2012-2016), Robots in Disguise 2015, and related novels.

data.name: Cyberverse
data.notes: Cyberverse animated series (2018-2021). Standalone continuity blending lore from multiple prior series.

data.name: EarthSpark
data.notes: Transformers: EarthSpark (2022-present). Paramount+ animated series introducing Terran characters born on Earth.

data.name: Transformers One
data.notes: Transformers One (2024). Animated theatrical film depicting the pre-war origin of Optimus Prime and Megatron on Cybertron. Own continuity.

data.name: A Real American Hero
data.notes: Original 1982-1994 continuity. Encompasses Sunbow cartoon (1983-1986), DIC cartoon (1989-1992), Marvel Comics (1982-1994), and the complete 3.75" toy line. The definitive G.I. Joe continuity.

data.name: Sigma 6
data.notes: Anime-influenced 8" figure reboot (2005-2007). Distinct character designs with exaggerated proportions. Accompanied by an animated series on 4Kids TV.

data.name: Resolute
data.notes: Warren Ellis adult-oriented web miniseries (2009). Grittier, more lethal take on ARAH characters where characters die permanently.

data.name: Renegades
data.notes: Hub Network animated series (2010-2011). A-Team inspired premise with the Joe team framed as fugitives. Modernized character designs.

data.name: G.I. Joe Movies
data.notes: Live-action film series. The Rise of Cobra (2009), Retaliation (2013), Snake Eyes (2021). Realistic military designs with sci-fi elements.
